title: Make each visible facet one coherent dimension
statement: Visible filters must represent comparable dimensions; mixed capability, market, and business-model terms belong in searchable metadata rather than one facet.
rationale: A facet becomes confusing when its options answer different classification questions.

prefer: Give each visible facet one mutually understandable classification dimension.
Keep useful cross-cutting terms searchable as metadata.
avoid: Mixing capability, business model, market, and context in one filter.

when: Users browse or filter a typed collection.
not_when: A small flat set has no meaningful facets.

evidence.summary: Rejected filters that mixed capability, business model, and market context; requested a coherent search taxonomy.

confidence.note: Bootstrapped from independent direct user episodes under the owner's request to mine durable design rules.

lifecycle.decision_note: Candidate pending independent recurrence or explicit owner approval.

positive_examples: Give each visible facet one mutually understandable classification dimension.
Keep useful cross-cutting terms searchable as metadata.
negative_examples: Mixing capability, business model, market, and context in one filter.

checks: Do all options answer the same question?
Would any option fit equally in another facet?
Can cross-cutting terms remain searchable without becoming filters?
